criterion: Quality and efficiency of the implementation

sub_sections.title: Work plan and resources
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO is structured as 9 work packages over 48 months in three functional layers: research pillar WPs (WP2 ATU, WP3 BIIS, WP4 CERIA, M1–M36), sectoral demonstrator WPs (WP5 NIHS, WP6 ISIA, WP7 ELI, M12–M42), and integration and dissemination (WP8 FIIT M6–M48, WP9 BAL full duration), supported throughout by WP1 (ATU, full duration). WP2 develops the neuro-symbolic planning engine across T2-01 (M1–12), T2-02 (M6–24), and T2-03 (M18–36), delivering D2-01 (M18) and D2-02 (M36). WP3 delivers the tri-store memory architecture via T3-01 (M1–12), T3-02 (M6–24), T3-03 (M18–30), producing D3-01 (M12) and D3-02 (M30). WP4 delivers formally specified coordination protocols via T4-01 (M3–15), T4-02 (M12–24), T4-03 (M18–36), producing D4-01 (M18) and D4-02 (M36). Demonstrator WPs each follow a use-case definition phase (M12–18), deployment phase (M18–36), and validation phase (M36–42), reflecting their confirmed dependence on WP2–WP4 outputs. WP8 delivers D8-01 (M12), D8-02 (M42), and D8-03 (M48); WP9 delivers D9-01 (M6), D9-02 (M36), and D9-03 (M48). The dependency map (wp_structure.json) contains 16 confirmed data-input edges; the representative critical path runs T2-01→T2-02→T2-03→T5-01→T5-02→T5-03→T8-03. Eleven milestones track progress from MS1 (Architecture specification finalised, M3) through MS11 (Project completion, M48); MS8 (Research pillar targets achieved, M36) and MS9 (Demonstrator validation complete, M42) are the principal scientific quality checkpoints. The risk register identifies 11 risks; highest-severity items are RISK-01 (planning/LLM integration, medium likelihood, high impact; mitigation: modular architecture and fallback modes, integration testing from M6), RISK-04 (clinical dataset access, medium likelihood, high impact; mitigation: ethics applications at M1 and data abstraction layer), RISK-09 (partner departure, low likelihood, high impact; mitigation: deputy PI appointments and consortium agreement commitment clauses), and RISK-10 (cross-pillar integration, medium likelihood, high impact; mitigation: shared API contracts defined M1–M3 and 6-monthly integration checkpoints). Consortium governance comprises three bodies: the General Assembly (all 8 partners, biannual) for strategic decisions; the Project Management Committee (all 8 partners, quarterly) for operational monitoring; and the Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee (ATU and NIHS, minimum biannual) for ethics and quality oversight. Ethics self-assessment triggers three categories: Category 8 (AI systems in safety-relevant domains, WP2/WP4/WP5/WP6), Category 4 (health data processing, WP5), and Category 2 (clinical expert involvement, WP5 T5-03). ATU holds the Ethics Compliance Coordinator role; NIHS holds the Healthcare Ethics Oversight role.

sub_sections.title: Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole
sub_sections.content: The 8-partner consortium spans 8 countries (DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, CH, FI, ES), combining higher education institutions, research centres, and private organisations with complementary expertise mapped directly to each WP. Alpenstadt Technical University (ATU, DE) coordinates and leads WP1 and WP2, contributing world-class expertise in neuro-symbolic AI, automated planning, and LLM-based reasoning. Centre Européen de Recherche en Informatique Avancée (CERIA, FR) leads WP4, providing formal multi-agent coordination protocol expertise and game-theoretic approaches to distributed AI cooperation. Breda Institute for Intelligent Systems (BIIS, NL) leads WP3, contributing pioneering work in retrieval-augmented generation and neural memory architectures directly addressing the topic's memory and state management requirements. Istituto Superiore di Ingegneria Avanzata (ISIA, IT) leads WP6, providing manufacturing domain knowledge, digital twin capability, and an operational pilot factory environment for validation. Nordic Institute of Health Sciences (NIHS, SE) leads WP5, contributing clinical decision support research expertise and access to de-identified clinical datasets with established validation pathways. EuroLog International AG (ELI, CH) leads WP7, providing real-world logistics operations data and multi-actor supply chain environments across international corridors. Boreal AI Labs Oy (BAL, FI) leads WP9, contributing LLM fine-tuning, open-source European AI development, and AI-on-demand platform integration expertise. Fundación Ibérica de Innovación Tecnológica (FIIT, ES) leads WP8, contributing AI evaluation framework design, benchmarking methodology, and EDIH network management for technology transfer. Each partner holds exactly one WP lead role matched to their primary competence, with WP lead assignments confirmed in wp_structure.json (Tier 4). The consortium covers healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and logistics — the Apply AI sectors identified in SR-07 — alongside cross-sector generalisation. Resource allocation is consistent with the validated budget response covering all 9 WPs and all 8 partners with no blocking inconsistencies.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: 9 WPs with confirmed lead partner assignments
16 data-input dependency edges in dependency map
11 milestones MS1–MS11 with due months and verifiable criteria
Risk register containing 11 risks with likelihood, impact, and mitigation
Governance: General Assembly (biannual), PMC (quarterly), EQOC (min. biannual)
8 partners with legal names and short names
8 partners span 8 distinct countries (DE, FR, NL, IT, SE, CH, FI, ES)
Deliverable IDs, titles, and due months (D1-01 through D9-03)
SR-07 Apply AI sector coverage: healthcare, manufacturing, logistics
Budget gate passed; no budget figures cited beyond gate confirmation
WP lead roles confirmed from wp_structure.json (Tier 4 governs over Tier 3)
